flashcards.back: A linear collection of elements stored in contiguous memory locations, accessible via index positions.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of array random access?
flashcards.back: O(1) time complexity for accessing any element using index calculation.
2D arrays organize data in rows and columns, requiring two indices for access (matrix structure).
Storage technique where 2D array elements are stored row-wise in contiguous memory.

flashcards.front: What operation has O(n) time complexity in 1D arrays?
flashcards.back: Insertion/deletion at beginning/middle as it requires shifting elements.

flashcards.front: What is the formula for 2D array element address calculation?
flashcards.back: Base_address + (i * columns + j) * element_size for row-major order.
No per-element overhead as all elements share same data type and size.

flashcards.front: What is a common application of 2D arrays?
flashcards.back: Image processing (pixel matrices) and spreadsheet applications.
All elements must be of the same data type.
Process of accessing each element exactly once using loops.
Array that automatically resizes itself when capacity is exceeded.
Storage method where 2D array elements are stored column-wise in memory.
